channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Another stablecoin just RUGGED — 30 replies — 120.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Another stablecoin just RUGGED

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-26 16:28 UTC

OP

MIM (Magic Internet Money) stablecoin just rugged.
So many stablecoins are rugging right now it's crazy

Early replies (first 3)

62408562
IT has just begun

62408566
magic internet money still exists?

62408598
>>62408557
amazing they're even still around

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408623
Defi really is dead

62408804
>>62408566
It's an old ss

62408875
>>62408804
no it isn't, this happened today

62409989
>>62408557
Who?

62410014
>>62408557
Wow so stable

62411316
would pay $100 to watch an interview between caleb hammer and the largest magic internet money bagholder

62411344
>>62408557
That's when you know the bear is getting serious and there will be major dumps until we hit the bottom.

62414655
>>62408557
Last time I farmed this was almost a year ago, the APY would frequently go to zero and they would have to fucking manually approve more emissions via multisig kek. The writing was on the wall for a long time

Now they've deleted the discord server after rugging everyone

62414658
>>62414655
I'm actually amazed they were still running this and never caught any legal flak for the role they played in the terraluna ponzi scheme and collapse

62414664
this one MIGHT be a slurper
hold on

62414718
>>62414664
The shit people will buy instead of Bitcoin. Just lmfao. Sad really.

62414728
so depending on the collat that they start hiking rates on... this might re-peg
might

62414746
most likely won't re-peg
>reason:
their depreciated cauldrons is where most of the dogshit collat likely is
even if liquidated, they might not cover for a full repeg

62414751
>>62414664
Why would I want to repay my debt?

62414752
>>62414655
>manually approve more emissions via multisig
LMAO

62414758
>>62414751
dudes who borrowed from this might be better off letting liquidation happen than getting their dogshit collat back
only God knows what fuckery might be in the "depreciated cauldrons"

62414805
>>62414664
I will not be buying any but if you buy and it repairs, sell it the second it does.
>>62414718
>Buying bitcoin
Lmfao Buy real coins with actual utility.

62414828
>>62414805
I won't buy, this shit has thin liquidity
people might panic and send it to fractions of a cent just for the fuck of it
of course, at that point everyone is going to crowd and buy
but right now, it just looks like something went structurally wrong
and by what that guy posted, considering this shit is managed by fucking multisigs that put rates manually into the contracts...
it kinda does

62414831
>>62414805
>if you buy and it repairs

How’s the weather in Bangladesh?

62414841
>>62414831
Reread what I wrote retard. I said I will not buy it but encouraged him to not baghold the exit repeg if it happens because it never recovers and stays. You are so cooked by doom scrolling reels it is insane. You are incapable of reading a single sentence with patience for comprehension.

62414846
>>62414828
Maybe it will have a bounce somewhere along the line like UST did but do not bag hold it hoping for complete par recovery. Look at the LUNC generals in the archive if you want to feel the blood drain from your face imagining being them (and link is being outperformed by fucking LUNC, if you want to REALLY imagine being a bagholder of the deepest kind lol).

62414884
Why are you retards still in crypto??

62415000
>>62408557
usdc and tether can also depeg, then it's truly over
they can kill crypto at any time they want, wouldn't take more than the epstein class seizing circle and tether or their reserves
their buddies would all sell first, but this is a card they can and will play eventually if the goyim get too powerful and you will lose everything

62416138
>>62414841
>>62414846
Nice double post, yeah it takes about two seconds of reading your gibberish (which no one else will ever reply to because you’re brown and your opinion doesn’t matter) to tell you’re ESL as fuck and should GTFO

62416716
>>62415000
>usdc and tether can also depeg
they can only market depeg
their collat is cash equivalent, literally impossible to depeg unless the US government ceases to exist

62416862
>>62414658
Did the people behind MIM play a role in that? I thought MIM was just used (among several other stablecoins) as an attack vector on Curve to de-peg UST. Which we later found out (through a lawsuit) was allegedly done by Jane Street.

Unless I'm missing something then blaming the MIM creators for it is the same as blaming the USDT and USDC creators, which makes no sense

item.title: >only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable >now we know the price floor is $150 now is the time to buy SPCX — 28 replies — 112.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable >now we know the price floor is $150 now is the time to buy SPCX

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-26 16:31 UTC

OP

>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable
>now we know the price floor is $150
now is the time to buy SPCX

Early replies (first 3)

62408493
>>62408480
Bagholder cope.

62408507
>>62408493
No cope saar only gains to be made! Please buy more stocks from this very profitable company! Time is limited for maximum gains must hurry before money train takes off!

62408519
>>62408480
>>only down 4% from its ipo,
kek about to 50% in 6 months

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409551
>>62408480
>Has 9 days of data points.
>Yeah this is enough to predict the future.
Retards gonna retard.

62409673
>>62408480
t. Bought at 190 and panickingly trying to get others to hold his bags

62409699
>>62408480
Don't you realize 40% of the supply will be dumped in 2 months?

62409869
>>62408480
>buy my bags goy
no I don't think I will, I would consider buying stocks after the market corrects 50-75% from the current peak

62409872
>>62408480
IPO was $135

62409875
This is basically the perfect asset and the greatest IQ test in history. Ask yourself one question, do YOU want to fuck prostitutes on Mars?

62409882
>>62409875
id rather fuck them in my bedroom on earth. or my kitchen. or my living room...

62411812
>>62409551
>>62409673
>>62409699
>>62409872
150 is the floor

62411816
>>62408480
FLOOR BROS
WE'RE TESTING AGAIN

62411883
>>62409551
Yep. The insider shares haven't even been unlocked. Why is he in such a rush? Just wait. Massive supply coming in a couple of months. Michael Howell already says marginal liquidity in financial markets is lower.

Thus, SUPPLY++ & DEMAND??? = Likely lower price.

62414040
>>62408480
should I buy in now? I want to hold some before starship 13

62414121
>>62408480
with only 5% of the float available or w/e. holy shit yall are fucked when it opens up

62414137
>>62408480
Melon got re-bogged and fattened which he only does when things are going badly for him
2x short because of this

62414157
>>62408480
>We privatized NASA and now we want you to pay us to see Hollywood for scientists productions
Yeah, nah. I saw Kubrick admit to faking the moon landing.

62414178
>>62408480
There's genuinely not enough data to make a principled model of it. However the only thing not hypey about SpaceX is StarLink, which is a genuine business. Merging xAI into SpaceX however just tied a 1 ton ball and chain around it, because it's an insatiable blackhole of capex with no clear upside.

62415681
>>62414137
he was spending too much time being a master of POE2 instead of going to the gym. he'll get back on the horse.

62415707
This thread didn't age well.

62415715
>>62415707
$150 is still the floor. it'll be $300 by hanukkah

62415763
>>62411812
Holy baggie, this garbage isn't worth a fraction of what it is, why would your delusional number be even close to the floor

62416005
>>62408480
90x earnings is INSANE. Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity. Equivalent to buying Worldcomm with the exception that Space X probably has a longterm future, but is at absolute bubble top with an ABSURD valuation and no course to revenues in the near future, kept aloft by virtue of its multiple rules changed inclusion in indices receiving passive flows and retail hype that has come and gone.

62416247
>>62416005
>>62415763
it's already up for the day!

62416343
>>62416005
>Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity

while true, never underestimate the retardation of the retail masses

62416471
>>62408480
Keep buy Bagholders, keep buying!

62416737
>>62408480
I haven't seen a single bull "case" that has used numbers for buying Spcx. Meanwhile all Bears have to do is look at that those Quarterly and Y/Y financials directly below OP's image they were conveniently edited out.

62416873
>>62415707
Yep people of /biz/ seem to be unable to value this asset correctly. The SpaceMarines are back!!

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 160 replies — 84.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 160 • Updated: 2026-06-26 14:39 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags

62401765
I just wrote a super long post with all sorts of trade basics and an emphasis for this group to define normal position exit conditions so you can tangibly ask anons "did you exit before, during, or after the standardized 4chan-exit signals?" Along with recommending covered calls for slow moving but trend holding markets like gold/BTc/xmr or oil when watching forex markets. 10y+dxy are good to watch too.
Blah blah blah
But this whole thread degenerated into trying to sell "art", and not every anon working together for every group success to contribute to all that participate, but rather instead for every anon to work on their own project, by themselves, but as a group selling the same products/watering down their labor's value.

>>62401110
This is who you should ask for advice. This anon only lost 1% of his shit n that's if he's only got 1m. Not a big deal. Don't you anons want to be able to lose 10k casually n be unbothered yet responsible?

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62416357
Bump

item.title: VOO and Chill — 92 replies — 77.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-06-26 15:22 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409545
>>62400493
honestly everything is fucked. index fund bagholders are forced to buy shitstocks and act like paypiggies for pump and dumpers like elon musk. it's literally causing a bubble. nobody wins except billionaires who force your grandma's retirement fund to pay for their malfunctioning AI.

62409811
>low fee index funds
it's VTI and VXUS for me

62409901
>>62400636
lol doomer, stop spreading negativity. you will be a lot happier even if you just invested 150 a month.

62410053
>>62409901
>Realism is negativity!

62410067
>>62400493
wtf are you doing bringing common sense into my retard and bot zoo?

62410086
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
pop quiz! how much would you expect your money to grow over 150 months (12.5 years) with a conservative 6% annual return?

62410375
Yep just bought another share of VOO

62410405
>>62403053
They are also expecting SS and medicare to buff out their income and reduce their costs. Everyone here is likely planning to have no SS or other government pension.

62410414
>>62402995
>>62403044
Really you two are dumb as fuck. Do you not understand what FIRE is?

VOO has 15% CAGR since inception. 11.75% after inflation. With even a generous withdrawal rate you can live very comfortably on it if you have enough capital. My business partner does this, goes on vacations all the time. He has a couple of million invested across UK-equivalent ETFs like VUSA. Loads of people do this, it's not the absolute best use of capital but it's a good return for how liquid and low fee it is.
>>62403044
Learn to read you fucking imbecile. I posted that only millionaires CAN LIVE OFF IT. . Anyone can invest and make gains from ETFs but you won't get rich unless you invest for decades.
>Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this
The point of what? Investing? No it isn't. The point of ETFs? LOL. You are just some dumb teen shitcoiner with sky high time preference I can just tell. Go and buy more shitcoins so you can "get rich quick" dickhead.

62410968
>>62410414
This is why I just wanna get as much capital in there as soon as possible it just seems like something I can actually control versus chasing returns
Don’t have any ideas though

62410981
>>62407086
how about a job nigger

62411192
>>62410981
I am currently at my full time salary job right now silly

62412068
VOO bros are up bigly today

62413691
>>62412068
sweet

62413705
>>62400493
Felix and friends on youtube has a good way of putting it. Why invest in 500 companies when 50%+ are going down and sideways? If you have the time and money and a good strategy you can stick to you're better off picking stonks. But most people don't have the time, money and discipline to do so.

62413718
>>62413705
I think it's more like 20% of the s&p carries the other 80%, it is definitely lopsided. He (an ex investment banker) preaches buy and hold is a dead strategy.

62413734
>>62410053
Your "realism" doesn't sound realistic to people who actually do this stuff. It sounds retarded, like you've got no idea what you're actually talking about.

62413777
>>62413705
>>62413718
bc i cannot pick the winners, simple, look what happened today

62413963
If you don't just want to do VOO and chill only, just have a separate account at Vanguard that you treat like a savings account and deposit something there monthly to buy VOO or whatever. You'll be glad later on.

62414091
>>62412068
>checking your account daily
>investing only in one country

62414103
For me its 90% VOO
10% GPIQ
So i win even when I crab

62414142
>>62413705
>Felix and friends on youtube
>ex investment banker
>"Discover A Trading System That Makes Market Headlines Irrelevant"

62414162
>>62400493
Fuck VOO
>50% VT
>50% VTI
That's the real chill. Total U.S. investment is 81% of portfolio, you capture emerging markets, and some great companies you otherwise wouldn't have in VOO or VTI alone.

62414279
>>62414162
What’s the best way to compare the two? Track the CAGRs of VOO vs the average of VT and VTI combined

62414680
>>62414279
If you do that, its boomer tier analysis, otherwise that analysis would dictate to just buy VOO or QQQ overlooking the real value of VT/I. VT is used as a hedge if there's an exodus from U.S. markets in the next 5-10 years. VTI is used as a hedge against an exodus from bloated tech in the next 5-10 years but the U.S is still supreme (likely the case). When investment eventually settles from AI bloat and only 3-5 companies out of the current hundreds trying to pursue AI development come out on top, investment will switch into a different sector. With these etfs, you dont have to worry about what the next big thing is, which is why its the ultimate hold. Zero think. Comfy returns and they trail VOO by total return over 10 years 309% vs 325%, VT and VTI both have lowe p/e which makes the more value and less risk. Buy, hold, DRIP, and sell when you want to. This is the long end of my portfolio, and my 401k and IRAs. This is shit I am not touching for 30-40 years. Some people in my firm freak out over market turmoil, im zen, where one bucket empties, another fills for me.

62414768
>>62413705
>Felix and friends
pic is the better Felix

62414776
>>62414162
vti+vxus is more tax efficient

62414829
>>62414776
Barely, but VXUS does have the pas-through tax credit, something that amounts to $100 per $100k invested, which is better, plus you can set your parameters for how much you want allocated to international. But have you ever had to explain to a 24yo HR roastie to set up 15% portfolio allocation of VTIAX/VXUS?
>HALF HERE HALF THERE IT CANT BE THIS FUCKING HARD
The headache is not worth the hassle.

62415002
>>62410414
VOO is not 15%, its 10%
>since inception
I don't give a shit, its such a low sample that it's nearly statistically irrelevant. Here is that data from 1900-2016. US Large is VOO

62416552
Damn Breh just got a $2200 check do I just invest it boringly or do something fun with it

item.title: In the last month, BTC is down 25% HYPE is down 0.5%. — 21 replies — 47.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
In the last month, BTC is down 25% HYPE is down 0.5%.

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-26 16:06 UTC

OP

In the last month, BTC is down 25%
HYPE is down 0.5%.

Yeah I think I know what I'll be holding from now till the end of the next bullrun. Get a clue, will you?

Early replies (first 3)

62412249
>>62412162
Something to think about

62412308
hype can flip eth
the seethe this would cause is immense
it would actually help ethereum overall, the retarded voices who egotrip all the time would be silenced
bankless dudes would quit
trent would delete his account
anthroposissy would jump off a bridge

62412369
>>62412249
Yeah, im thinkin that Hype is barely down while Daddy BTC is shitting the bed. Are you daft or just a nigger?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412583
>>62412369
Cope chigger hype back to 3$

62412648
I told you many moons ago but none of you listened. HYPE is a coin that it's gonna be really hard to make it dump hard.

Fees are used for buybacks. And everytime there is a happening, the hyperliquid blockchain is more used, happenings sometimes mean people trading commodities, stocks, whatever. Now there's an outcome markets, people are betting on the stupid worldcup among other things.

It's one of the few blockchains that is actually used. Who knows what will happen, it could get hacked, who the fuck knows, but right now, if things keep going like this, it will surpass ethereum.

62412734
>>62412648
It's crazy how shit biz has become. These brownoids have no foresight.

62412740
>>62412162
20% circulated.

62412786
>>62412740
it's very obvious you dont know anything about the tokenomics. I wont bother explaining here cuz you're all poor low IQ brownies anyway

62412822
>>62412786
Ok saar hyderabad very good coin, aped lifesavings blody!

62412829
CZ bought the presidential pardon and still has $100B to buy other favors. Jeff will meet the same fate as SBF.

62412847
>>62412162
>Get a clue, will you?
Why are there so many super obvious indians on /biz/ all of a sudden?

62412895
>>62412847
Indians dont post on /biz/ anymore tardie, they've been on twitter for 3 years now

62412914
Hype good coin saaarrrr
Fuck off faggot shills!

62412929
>>62412914
Lol indian coins are holding the line while BTC shit coin is dumping like PISS. DRINK UP THE PISS BLOODY BENCHOD

62412985
>>62412786
i hope all .hl die a painful death honestly, worse than pump trenchers
.

62414285
Hype is still higher than when btc was 120k

hyperliquid

62416604
Hype simply refuses to dump.

62416668
>>62416604
Jeets actually care about their coins, unlike these whitu piggu BTC niggers.

62416713
Silver's down nearly as much, it's lost 50% from peak. But silver's an actual thing that exists. I'd much rather ride it back up as this house of cards finally falls.

62416734
>>62416668
hyperliquid

62416762
>>62416713
go back to PMG loser

item.title: Is Take Two Interactive stock a good buy right now? — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is Take Two Interactive stock a good buy right now?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-26 16:27 UTC

OP

Is Take Two Interactive stock a good buy right now? Currently trading at 236 per share, slight drop in price due to pre-order controversy with GTA VI. How much do you think it can go up to when it launches in November?

Early replies (first 3)

62416803
only if you're a nigger
if you encourage console exclusivity you are a nigger. do not give these people money or attention. to me, gta 5 doesn't even exist and you could pay me to play the free copy i have on epic and i wouldn't do it

62416830
>>62416780
I'm betting on GTA VI being a flop or at least under performing. If anything, take two's stock will go down over the course of GTA VI's life time if they just double down on the online shit. the game will be ultimately unsatisfying and the playerbase will bleed out even if initial sales are good.

62416843
Who the fucks buys a Ps5 or xbox nowawdays? This game will flop, price too high.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62416849
>>62416843
Im going to be a janitor on gt6 make a honest living.

62416857
The game will sell like crazy but be the begining of the end for the series. All the original Rockstar guys are gone now. There will be so many changes from formula and propaganda that it will rub too many people the wrong way. There probably won't be a GTA 7. This stock may be good for a pump and dump, but I'm not buying. Too risky at too high a price already.

item.title: WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling — 7 replies — 15.8/hr

you can sell now at 70

62416754
>>62416450
They are actually 1 week away from losing pressure. Iran knows this and is in no rush.

62416764
>>62416450

item.title: Why do goycattle not understand business? — 7 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do goycattle not understand business?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-26 15:48 UTC

OP

Why do goycattle not understand business? This dude has so much private wealth from his scams that even if BTC goes to zero tomorrow he has many hundreds of millions of dollars that can't be taken away from him. He does NOT have as much skin in the game as goycattle think.

Early replies (first 3)

62415324
>>62415314
is that your example of someone that understands business? Something like 1/2 of americans will start their own business at some point.
Of those, about 1/4 will succeed and become rich.

so no, most people understand business just fine. Not billionaires, but far richer than the average poster here.

62416020
>>62415314
Agreed. He did the same exact thing in Dotcom. His stock dumped 99%. It is currently down like 90% and sure to complete the 99. His preferred equity dividend STRC is in a death spiral. He will be sued BIGLY this time and BTC will lose its top spot just like it did in 2018 but in a more lasting, narrative breaking way, and the world will be DRAMATICALLY better for it. Buy good coins when no one is saying to at the bottom which we are not REMOTELY close to yet.

62416502
>>62415314
He's effectively running a ponzi scheme. When it goes bankrupt the law will be coming after him and any money he has left.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62416558
>>62415314
i'd love to see what his private investments are in. most likely Tbills ,bond etfs and extremely conservative index funds and like 2% in crypto lol

you retards need to start looking at what the wealthy are invested in, not what they're telling you to invest in.

62416565
Bunch of retards, you probably haven't even researched BTC for more than 24 hours

62416583
>>62416502
It isn't a ponzi scheme please stay on reddit. His stock is down because btc is down it isn't hard to understand. A ponzi has no underlying asset or business structure, it's literally just taking money and giving it to early investors. The irony is that new investors in mstr/btc right now will probably get much better performance than baggies.

62416659
>>62416020
What is your guess for bottoms? My guesses are
$18k btc and $900 ETH because of the massive psychological doom going below 2017 top would cause for Normies. They’d all hate crypto and believe it to be dead forever if this happened

item.title: >Help me make a DCA plan I have 55k USDT, 0.1618 BTC, 1.25 BNB, 1 SOL and 0.09 ETH This is my only chance to make it >in… — 2 replies — 1.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Help me make a DCA plan I have 55k USDT, 0.1618 BTC, 1.25 BNB, 1 SOL and 0.09 ETH This is my only chance to make it >in…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-26 14:56 UTC

OP

>Help me make a DCA plan

I have 55k USDT, 0.1618 BTC, 1.25 BNB, 1 SOL and 0.09 ETH
This is my only chance to make it
>inb4 only 55k USDT
that's a lot of money in my local currency

I'm planning to allocate like this:
BTC - 38,500 USDT
ETH - 8,250 USDT
SOL - 5,500 USDT
BNB - 2,750 USDT

I'm planning to buy like this:
BTC DCA:
55k - 2,500
50k - 3,000
45k - 3,500
40k - 4,500
35k - 5,000
30k - 6,000
25k - 7,000
20k - 7,000

ETH DCA:
1450 - 750
1300 - 850
1150 - 1,000
1000 - 1,150
850 - 1,300
700 - 1,500
550 - 1,700

SOL DCA:
65 - 500
55 - 600
45 - 700
35 - 900
25 - 1,100
20 - 1,700

BNB DCA:
500 - 250
450 - 300
400 - 400
350 - 500
300 - 600
250 - 700

If the prices never go this low for all coins I'll just dump their remaining allocation into them after they start going up after the bottom

Is there a flaw in the plan?

Don't tell me to buy any other coins because I can't focus on more things at once

Early replies (first 3)

62414356
go all in on live cattle

62416427
well?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

OP

62410760
i mean he looks like a sojack, thats kind of on you

62413165

62413241

Give context you nigger

62413259
>>62413241

>Assblaster was a fraud
This was always obvious

62413262
>>62413241
https://www.coinmetro.com/blog/announcement-onboarding-depositing-and-withdrawals
There are people in their telegram group talking about roping as a result of this. I'm not joking

62413573
>>62413262
So another exchange shutting down and stealing all users funds?

62414199
>>62413262
The ama was hard to watch. The amount of times he had to say he can't guarantee people's funds and that it's up to the courts was irritating. He kept saying they are all in it together and that he was too as in investor, ceo and user of the platform. I bet he didn't halt his own withdrawals though. What a fuckin goon on the whole "reorganization" pitch. Hopefully karma gets that stupid mother fucker. His body language said it all.

62414273
it's over. the sec need to be restored so it can go after every single piece of shit crypto project and exchange

62414281
>>62410743
>what if we had banks but like there we no rules about how the bankers behaved
Truly amazing how anyone thought this wasn't a scam.

62414293
>>62414281
Libertardian dream

62414317
>>62414199
>>62413262
Not your keys, not your crypto. Does remind me of last cycle when Kucoin closed up though. Hmm…

62414659
>>62410743

62414772
imagine trusting a man with a hairline like that? couldnt be me

62414835
>>62410743
My 1 whole btc is on there. I procrastinated putting it my own wallet. Are they scaming or we can get it back?

62414898
How the fuck does an exchange go bankrupt without a huge hack? Its like Trump bankrupting a casino. It should not happen

62414931
>>62410743
tfw a personal friend of mine for years started a crypto coin that actually took off when musk mentioned it a few years ago. it mooned and he sold all he held to pay off his house. black lives matter-tier scammer. you literally cannot trust anyone.

62415834
>>62410743
>NO ONE is trustworthy in crypto
No one is trustworthy. Cursed is the man who trusts in man.

62415841
Ver1zon hires the most pedophiles in the state of new jersey. Even with charges they keep their job touching kids that changes today.

62416152
>>62414317
True. As for Kucoin, I don't think they ever filed for chapter 11. They did cease operations in the US because I do remember them telling users to withdraw all of their holdings. Wasn't the fud around Tera Luna and Kucoin's supposed exposure to it? I think that was a rumor: https://www.kucoin.com/blog/be-aware-of-fud

62416320
>>62413262
>There are people in their telegram group talking about roping as a result of this. I'm not joking

62416840

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 85 replies — 66.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-06-26 15:16 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62411241
>>62396002
What About gold and silver?

62411256
>>62404304
I'm not convinced .

62411265
I will just keep my 0.3 btc somewhere

62411638
>>62395915
Institutions are getting onboard, It might do 10X in a decade, also lending is starting to become more relevant with the likes of TBV, there are so many utilities untapped.

62412797
>>62411160
go to work

62413082
>>62411638
That's the part most people miss. BTC doesn't have to just sit in a wallet forever.

62413193
>>62406783
YOU'RE RETARD

62414853
>>62409813
Good idea

62414893
>>62395655
>Why aren't you holding BTC?
because there is this one nigger saylormoon who hold nearly a million of these, only boughted with debts & baggies hopes and dreams. MSTR alone was most of the buying pressure the past 2 years. What would the price look like to your opinion if it wasn't for Mike buying more than all the BTC mined worldwide each year?
We would already be back in the 15k range if it wasn't for him throwing infinite monies at BTC. And now his preferential dividend model is failing, do you really believe MSTR will be able to continue the purchase frenzy?
In just 6 months STRC went from 7 years worth of dividends to pay in their treasury down to 13 months. This boat is bleeding cash left and right.

62414918
>>62409051
Stolen from Sam Hyde

62414938
>>62414893
He knows what's he's doing

62414950
>>62413082
It won't work forever

62414955
>>62414944
How?

62415214
>>62414950
Hmmm, that's why it's still in public testnet instead of being pushed straight into production. I'm more interested in where it could lead than writing it off this early.

62415217
>>62414893
MSTR has definitely been a major buyer, but I don't think Bitcoin's entire value proposition depends on one company continuing to buy forever.

62415228
>>62395655
Because I have no brown, black or asian admixture.

62415296
>>62395655
Bitcorn has no liquidity now - there are no baggies left to trap.

I'd rather gamble on stocks - at least the price actually moves.

62415297
>>62396879
There is 'demand' for literally meme garbage like PEPE and DOGE which do nothing - not really an argument.

62415301
>>62415217
No, but MSTR collapse will cause a negative doom loop for Bitcorn if they have to start dumping.

62415492
>>62415228
Are you white?

62416132
>>62395655
I sold between 90-110k
Bought back between 59-64k
Still 1/3 of Tether left to buy further dips

62416334
>>62411213
Yeah. The Aegis partnership makes me even more interested to see how the TBV public testnet progresses.

62416338
>>62415301
but short term

62416355
>>62416132
Nice trades.

62416385
>>62395655
because I make money trading not holding garbage

62416439
>>62416385
Because I believe Bitcoin is the strongest long-term store of value in crypto. Short-term volatility doesn't change why I bought it in the first place.

62416488
>>62411265
I'm not holding bitcoin for next week. I'm holding it for the next decade.

62416497
>>62411213
I have doubts for Aave coin

62416506
Because every major dip has looked scary in the moment, but the long-term trend is what matters to me.

62416528
eistein coin

62415855
>>62409326

The people doing that are buying from mining companies now and not LE SILVER ENDS THE FED POSTING. They are actually doing the work to educate people on what a alt economy is offline.

item.title: What the actual FUCK is this? — 26 replies — 18.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
What the actual FUCK is this?

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-26 15:10 UTC

OP

What the actual FUCK is this?

Early replies (first 3)

62410973
>>62410915
Thank you for playing

62411210
They just did their AMA but banned any questions related to:

- The token
- Revenue
- Unlocks
- Buy backs
- The reserve
- Staking
- Timelines

They basically just responded to a bunch of questions like "We know Chainlink is the industry standard and already being adopted by major institutions but how can the community help"
Follow by answers like
"Talk to your friends and family to spread awareness, follow us on social media and share our posts"

It was just an excuse for them to ask holders to shill LINK to their family so they can get more liquidity to dump the 18 million tokens that unlocked for them this week

62411645
>>62411210
Buy our tokens so we can keep making these 'patnership' headlines that you read on secondary market (4chan)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62411772
>>62410915
A visual representation of how Chainlink Labs is in fact ABOVE the chainlink TOKEN.
The link token will continue its downward trajectory into irrelevance

62411846
>>62411210
brutal.

t. i own 565 links tokwens

62411852
>>62411645
jesus fuking christ

62411855
>>62410973
i really did miss another bullrun didnt i .


i promise to god im selling it all next bull run when we hit 17 dollars, cant sell now... too late for that.

62412054
>>62411855
TNN

You really fell for a bunch of buzzwords and low tier shilling by schizophrenic baggies kek

62412075
>>62410915
Aaaaaaah it's over!!
I should have listened to the fuddies! I should have sold and bought xrp instead! Aaaaaaaaa!

62412108
>>62412054
yup i wasted 4 years of my life invesnting to get like 600 link tokens

62412114
>>62412075
And here we see a link marine being unable to comprehend that he and the xrp army are two faces of the same retarded coin.

62412123
>>62412114
Nooooo! I should have bought BTC! AAAAAAAAAA

62412174
>>62412123
No, you should continue to buy chainlink as it sinks to zero. Sergey really appreciates your donations.

62412690
>>62411210

>- Revenue
>- Unlocks
>- Buy backs
>- The reserve
>- Staking
>- Timelines
Absolutely brvtal, jeetlinkers have been thoroughly raped.

62413749

62413788

62413835
>>62413788

summer is always the worst time for markets and summer only just started

62413846
>>62413835

62413973

62413986
>>62413973

62414045
>>62411210

62414156
>>62413973
I hate that this picture looks a lot like me. I wish I never fucking bought this coin.

62416065
>>62411210
Amazingly transparent retail liquidity milking findom scheme.
>>62414045
Do you even hear yourself or begin to consider what you're becoming or sacrificing for sake of defending this coin to signal consensus to the low IQ greed cult? Seriously, is it just self-hatred the way a gambler aspires to lose everything unconsciously? Why be like this or cope in this absurd of a way instead of just admitting you were wrong?

62416073
>>62411855
Try 1.70 dollars.

62416464
alright fuck it yeah, it was and still is a scam
you were right the whole time

62416492

Why didn't you listen?
You could have prevented this.
It's too late.
It's over. OGRE.
